"C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe" -Command "python -m pytest tests/polaris_graph/test_atom_refusal_validator.py --basetemp C:\\tmp\\codex_atom_iter3_review" in C:\POLARIS
 succeeded in 890ms:
  88:     re.IGNORECASE,
  89: )
  90: 
  91: # Iter-2 fix (Codex iter-1 P2): inclusion/eligibility-range framing.
  92: # "Eligible patients had inclusion criteria of HbA1c between 7.0 and 10.0"
  93: # is design/eligibility, not an outcome claim. This regex detects framing
  94: # that overrides Trigger A for eligibility-range numbers.
  95: #
  96: # Iter-3 fix (Codex iter-2 novel-P1): tightened to only "inclusion/
  97: # exclusion criteria" frames; removed bare "baseline HbA1c" because real
  98: # outcome sentences may mention baseline characteristics + actual
  99: # reductions in the same breath ("Patients with baseline HbA1c of 8.6%
 100: # had HbA1c reductions of 2.3 percentage points" must NOT bypass Trigger A).
 101: _ELIGIBILITY_RANGE_RE = re.compile(
 102:     r"\b(?:inclusion\s+criter\w*|exclusion\s+criter\w*|"
 103:     r"eligibility\s+criter\w*|eligible\s+(?:if|when)\b|"
 104:     r"required\s+(?:hba1c|weight|bmi)\s+(?:of|between)\b)",
 105:     re.IGNORECASE,
 106: )
 107: 
 108: # Iter-3 fix (Codex iter-2 novel-P1): outcome-verb-with-number pattern.
 109: # If a sentence contains "<outcome verb> <amount>" (reductions of N,
 110: # decreased by N, improved by N), the eligibility override MUST NOT fire
 111: # because there's a real quantitative outcome claim mixed with the
 112: # eligibility framing.
 113: _OUTCOME_VERB_WITH_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(
 114:     r"\b(?:reduc(?:ed|tions?|ing)|"
 115:     r"decreas(?:ed|es?|ing)|increas(?:ed|es?|ing)|"
 116:     r"chang(?:ed|es?)|improv(?:ed|ements?|ing)|"
 117:     r"lower(?:ed|ing)|rais(?:ed|ing)|"
 118:     r"fell|rose|dropped)\s+"
 119:     r"(?:by|of|from|to)?\s*[-−]?\d",
 120:     re.IGNORECASE,
 121: )
 122: 
 123: # Qualitative comparative/outcome language requiring atom citation
 124: # (Codex iter-1 P1 expanded: catch "more common with X than Y", "higher
 125: # with X than Y", "greater reduction than", etc.)
 126: _QUAL_COMPARATIVE_RE = re.compile(
 127:     r"\b("
 128:     r"greater(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
 129:     r"less(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
 130:     r"lower(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
 131:     r"higher(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
 132:     r"more(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"     # "more common with X than Y"
 133:     r"fewer(?:\s+\w+){0,4}\s+than|"
 134:     r"superior(?:ity)?\s+to|non[-\s]?inferior(?:ity)?\s+to|"
 135:     r"statistically\s+significant|significantly\s+\w+|"
 136:     r"reduced|increased|decreased|elevated|improved|worsened|"
 137:     r"more\s+(?:effective|common|frequent)|less\s+(?:effective|common|frequent)|"
 138:     r"compared\s+(?:to|with)|versus|vs\.?"
 139:     r")\b",
 140:     re.IGNORECASE,
 141: )
 142: 
 143: # Comparator/arm language that — combined with a comparative phrase —
 144: # signals a factual comparative claim even without a specific endpoint
 145: # vocab term. Iter-3 fix (Codex iter-2 P2): tightened from `\w{3,}` to
 146: # specific treatment-arm terms only. "than enough", "than expected",
 147: # "than usual" no longer match.
 148: _COMPARATIVE_ARM_RE = re.compile(
 149:     r"\b(?:than|versus|vs\.?|compared\s+(?:to|with))\s+"
 150:     r"(?:"
 151:     r"placebo|control(?:s|\s+arm)?|standard\s+care|usual\s+care|"
 152:     r"active\s+comparator|background\s+therapy|sham|"
 153:     # Drug-class terms
 154:     r"glp[-\s]?1|sglt2|dpp[-\s]?4|insulin|metformin|sulfonylurea|"
 155:     # Common comparator drug names (subset of atom_extractor's _DRUG_RE)
 156:     r"semaglutide|tirzepatide|dulaglutide|liraglutide|"
 157:     r"empagliflozin|dapagliflozin|canagliflozin|ertugliflozin|"
 158:     r"sitagliptin|linagliptin|saxagliptin|alogliptin|"
 159:     r"warfarin|apixaban|rivaroxaban|dabigatran|edoxaban|"
 160:     r"rosuvastatin|atorvastatin|simvastatin"
 161:     r")\b",
 162:     re.IGNORECASE,
 163: )
 164: 
 165: # Numbers to EXCLUDE from Trigger B when they appear with admin/design context
 166: _ADMIN_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(
 167:     r"\b("
 168:     r"phase\s+(?:I{1,3}V?|IV|\d+)|"
 169:     r"week[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+week[s]?|"
 170:     r"month[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+month[s]?|"
 171:     r"year[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+year[s]?|"
 172:     r"day[s]?\s+\d+|\d+\s+day[s]?|"
 173:     r"n\s*=\s*\d+|sample\s+size\s+\d+|"
 174:     r"arm\s+\d+|\d+\s+arm[s]?|"
 175:     r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:mg|μg|mcg|kg|g|mL|L|U|IU)(?!\s*/[dl])"  # dose-only, lab unit still triggers
 176:     r")\b",
 177:     re.IGNORECASE,
 178: )
 179: 
 180: # Allowed-without-atom categories — narrative/synthesis prose
 181: _NARRATIVE_CATEGORY_RE = re.compile(
 182:     r"\b("
 183:     r"mechanism\s+of\s+action|receptor\s+agonis[mt]|incretin|"
 184:     r"open[-\s]?label|double[-\s]?blind|randomi[zs]ed|placebo[-\s]?controlled|"
 185:     r"phase\s+(?:I{1,3}V?|IV|\d+)\s+(?:trial|study)|"
 186:     r"inclusion\s+criter|exclusion\s+criter|eligibility|eligible\s+patients|"
 187:     r"prevalence|epidemiology|burden\s+of\s+disease|"
 188:     r"limitation|limited\s+by|caveat|long[-\s]?term\s+\w+\s+(?:data|safety)|"
 189:     r"these\s+(?:outcomes|results|findings)\s+(?:were|are)\s+(?:consistent|in\s+line)"
 190:     r")\b",
 191:     re.IGNORECASE,
 192: )
 193: 
 194: 
 195: # Atom-ID citation pattern: atom_NNN
 196: _ATOM_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\batom_\d{3,}\b")
 197: _EV_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\[?ev_\d{3,}(?::\d+-\d+)?\]?")
 198: _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:ev_\d{3,}:\d+-\d+\]")
 199: 
 200: 
 201: @dataclass
 202: class GapRecord:
 203:     """One per sentence in a section's validation pass."""
 204:     section_id: str
 205:     section_title: str
 206:     sentence_index: int
 207:     original_sentence: str
 208:     rendered_text: str
 209:     action: RefusalAction
 210:     reason: RefusalReason
 211:     cited_atoms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 212:     missing_atoms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 213:     detected_endpoint: Optional[str] = None
 214:     detected_entity: Optional[str] = None
 215:     detected_timepoint: Optional[str] = None
 216:     detected_values: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 217:     notes: Optional[str] = None
 218: 
 219:     def to_dict(self) -> dict:
 220:         return {
 221:             "claim_id": f"{self.section_id}.s{self.sentence_index:03d}",
 222:             "sentence_index": self.sentence_index,
 223:             "original_sentence": self.original_sentence,
 224:             "rendered_text": self.rendered_text,
 225:             "action": self.action.value,
 226:             "reason": self.reason.value,
 227:             "cited_atoms": self.cited_atoms,
 228:             "missing_atoms": self.missing_atoms,
 229:             "detected_endpoint": self.detected_endpoint,
 230:             "detected_entity": self.detected_entity,
 231:             "detected_timepoint": self.detected_timepoint,
 232:             "detected_values": self.detected_values,
 233:             "notes": self.notes,
 234:         }
 235: 
 236: 
 237: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 238: # Quantitative-claim detection
 239: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 240: 
 241: def requires_atom_citation(sentence: str) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str]]:
 242:     """Codex APPROVE_DESIGN claim detector.
 243: 
 244:     Returns (requires_atom, reason). True if sentence makes a factual
 245:     quantitative claim that needs atom_NNN citation; False for pure
 246:     narrative/mechanism/trial-design prose.
 247: 
 248:     Triggers:
 249:       A: number + endpoint vocab term → requires atom
 250:       B: number alone (excluding admin/design numbers) → requires atom
 251:       Plus: qualitative comparative outcome language even without numbers
 252: 
 253:     Excludes:
 254:       - pure mechanism / trial identity / eligibility / background prose
 255:     """
 256:     s = sentence.strip()
 257:     if not s:
 258:         return False, None
 259: 
 260:     numbers = _NUMBER_RE.findall(s)
 261:     has_endpoint = bool(_ENDPOINT_VOCAB_RE.search(s))
 262:     has_qual_comparative = bool(_QUAL_COMPARATIVE_RE.search(s))
 263:     has_comparator_arm = bool(_COMPARATIVE_ARM_RE.search(s))
 264:     has_outcome_number = bool(numbers) and has_endpoint
 265: 
 266:     # Iter-2/3 fix (Codex iter-2 novel-P1): eligibility-range override
 267:     # ONLY fires when the sentence is PURE eligibility framing:
 268:     #   - has eligibility keyword
 269:     #   - no qualitative comparative
 270:     #   - no outcome-verb-with-number ("reductions of 2.3", "decreased
 271:     #     by 0.5", etc.)
 272:     # This prevents masking real outcome claims that happen to mention
 273:     # baseline characteristics.
 274:     if (
 275:         _ELIGIBILITY_RANGE_RE.search(s)
 276:         and not has_qual_comparative
 277:         and not _OUTCOME_VERB_WITH_NUMBER_RE.search(s)
 278:     ):
 279:         return False, None
 280:
